---
title: "Modelling Behaviour in Matching Pennies"
---

This site walks through fitting behavioural models to matching pennies data,
where a player repeatedly chooses Left or Right against an opponent and is
rewarded for matching (or mismatching) the opponent's choice.

We cover five notebooks:

1. **[Fundamentals](notebooks/00_fundamentals.qmd)** -- a primer on the Python
   syntax and machine learning concepts (log-likelihood, softmax,
   cross-validation) used throughout the tutorials, with links to where each
   concept appears in the code.
2. **[Synthetic Data](notebooks/01_synthetic_data.qmd)** -- generate choice
   sequences from known strategies (biased, win-stay-lose-switch, perseverant)
   that switch within sessions, so we have ground-truth data to validate the
   models.
3. **[GLM-HMM](notebooks/02_glm_hmm.qmd)** -- fit a Generalized Linear Model
   with Hidden Markov Model states to recover latent behavioural strategies.
4. **[Q-Learning](notebooks/03_qlearning.qmd)** -- fit a reinforcement-learning
   model (Lee et al.) that tracks action values trial by trial.
5. **[Model Comparison](notebooks/04_model_comparison.qmd)** -- compare
   GLM-HMM and Q-learning using within-session cross-validation on identical
   held-out trials.
